items.title: k-means and inertia
items.description: k-means minimizes inertia (WCSS) by construction; elbow plots and inertia curves are the natural first diagnostic after fitting Lloyd's algorithm.

items.title: Precision, recall, and F1
items.description: Supervised classification metrics score predicted labels against ground truth for a trained classifier. Cluster external metrics (ARI, NMI) score partition agreement without assuming a decision boundary—use the right metric family for the task.

items.title: Cross-validation and resampling
items.description: Stability checks—bootstrap refits, held-out label evaluation, and comparing metrics across seeds—borrow the same disciplined resampling mindset as cross-validation for reliable model comparison.

items.title: Experiment tracking
items.description: Log inertia, silhouette, ARI, and chosen k per run so algorithm and preprocessing comparisons remain auditable across experiments and team members.
